stories.headline: Anthropic just turned Claude into a physical button you can press
stories.blurb: Claude Tag is a $20 NFC sticker you tap with your phone to instantly start a voice conversation with Claude. No unlocking, no app-hunting — just tap and talk. It connects to your existing Claude account and works anywhere NFC does.
stories.why_it_matters: The fastest way to use AI just became as simple as tapping your phone on your desk.

stories.headline: Meta just launched smart glasses without Ray-Ban
stories.blurb: After three years of exclusively partnering with Ray-Ban, Meta is now selling smart glasses under its own brand. The new Meta Glasses come in three styles and seven colors, including a collaboration with Kylie Jenner. Pricing and specs weren't disclosed, but the move signals Meta's push to own the smart glasses category outright.
stories.why_it_matters: Meta is betting it can sell AI glasses on its own brand, not just Ray-Ban's.
stories.original_headline: Meta launches cheaper smart glasses without Ray-Ban

stories.headline: Cursor just got a settings page for all your AI plugins and tools
stories.blurb: Cursor's new Customize page brings plugins, skills, and MCPs (Model Context Protocol integrations) into one central hub. Instead of hunting through settings or docs, you can now see and manage all the ways you've extended your AI coding assistant in a single view.
stories.why_it_matters: Makes customizing your AI editor feel more like configuring VS Code than debugging config files.

stories.headline: Groq raises $650M after Nvidia paid $20B for its team but not the company
stories.blurb: AI chip startup Groq just closed a $650M round and hired new executives after Nvidia paid $20B to poach most of its staff without buying the business. The company is doubling down on its neocloud inference service, essentially rebuilding from scratch with fresh capital and leadership.
stories.why_it_matters: A $20B talent raid that leaves the company standing is wild — and rare proof a business model can survive losing most of its team.
stories.original_headline: AI chipmaker Groq confirms $650M raise, re-staffs after Nvidia’s $20B not-acqui-hire deal

stories.headline: Google DeepMind just invested $75M in A24 to build AI filmmaking tools
stories.blurb: DeepMind is partnering with indie studio A24 (Everything Everywhere All At Once, The Whale) to develop AI tools for actual film production. The $75M deal puts Google's AI research lab directly into Hollywood's creative pipeline.
stories.why_it_matters: AI is moving from marketing clips to real filmmaking with a studio that ships Oscar winners.
stories.original_headline: Google DeepMind bets $75M on AI’s future in Hollywood with A24 deal

try_this_prompt.title: The research rabbit hole escape
try_this_prompt.prompt: I'm researching a topic and I've collected way too much information. I'll describe what I'm trying to figure out below. Give me: the one question my research should answer, three sources I can skip entirely, and the single insight I probably already have but haven't noticed yet.
try_this_prompt.tool_hint: When you've read twelve articles and still can't start writing.

daily_question: If you could turn one AI tool into a physical button on your desk, what would it do?
